<!-- generated by harness — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: harness-build

- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.047Z — chose **BD1: Stack = TypeScript/Node ≥18 across CLI/MCP/shims, distributed via npm** over Rust/Go (slower to build in the window, MCP SDK maturity); Python (weak global-CLI distribution story) because MCP SDK is TS-first; users already have Node (Claude Code requires it); npm is the distribution channel; one language across CLI/MCP/shims
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.108Z — chose **BD2: Write-back enforcement = Stop hook (exit 2 blocks stop, forces continue), with stop_hook_active check guarding against infinite loops** over SessionEnd hook because SessionEnd is cleanup-only and cannot feed back to the agent; Stop can block and force the write-back
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.166Z — chose **BD3: Status projection injected at SessionStart is summary-dense; detail lives in per-session files** over (no alternative recorded) because the projection must fit the 10,000-char context cap
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.227Z — chose **BD4: Hooks are thin standalone scripts calling `harness event append` — logic lives in the CLI** over command logic inline in the hook scripts because shims stay portable across Claude Code/Codex when they carry no logic
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.287Z — chose **BD5: Events are truth; md files are generated projections — hand-edits to projections are a bug** over (no alternative recorded) because per the 03-architecture storage decision
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.346Z — chose **BD6: Schema (event payloads + projection templates) confined to src/schema/ and src/projections/templates/ (paths superseded by BD11: packages/schema/src/ and packages/engine/src/projections/templates/)** over (no alternative recorded) because Phase 0/1 schema iteration touches only these locations
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.409Z — chose **BD7: devDependencies typescript, esbuild, vitest, @types/node allowed as toolchain; runtime dependency set stays exactly @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, commander, chokidar, ulid** over (no alternative recorded) because toolchain is not runtime deps — the CLAUDE.md dependency rule itself names vitest/esbuild as the test/build tools
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.473Z — chose **BD8: Correction fold semantics — a correction event VOIDS the event its ref points at (target skipped during replay); replacement content is appended as a fresh event** over in-place patching because patching violates event immutability and complicates replay; SPEC only says "referencing the target id", so voiding is the minimal meaningful interpretation
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.536Z — chose **BD9: current.* fields are DERIVED at fold end, not stored — active_phase = first active phase; next_action = latest session_ended's next_action; blocked_on = blocked phases/tasks (blocking note when present, cleared on unblock); envelope-valid events of unknown type still advance the cursor** over storing redundant state-sync events in the log because SPEC leaves the computation unspecified; deriving keeps the log free of redundant state; sync moves events by envelope, not payload
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.597Z — chose **BD10: ulid generation uses monotonicFactory(), not the default ulid()** over default ulid() — randomly ordered within the same millisecond because random same-ms ordering breaks the cursor contract (creation order must match sort order for "events since id"); cross-process same-ms ordering stays unspecified, acceptable because fold replays in file order
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.657Z — chose **BD11: Monorepo now (user-directed, Jul 3) — npm workspaces, zero new tooling: packages/schema = @harness/schema (source-shipped internal package, no build step until it publishes); packages/engine = harness bin; SPEC §Repo layout + CLAUDE.md guard-rail paths updated; BD6 path references superseded** over deferring to v2 (the prior recommendation); pnpm/turborepo because user prefers paying the structure cost early so ui/sync/adapters slot in later; npm workspaces add no dependencies
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.718Z — chose **BD12: MCP server uses the SDK's LOW-LEVEL API (Server + setRequestHandler(ListTools/CallTool)) with hand-written plain-JSON-Schema tool definitions and @harness/schema validators (packages/schema/src/tool-inputs.ts)** over the high-level McpServer.registerTool API because registerTool wants zod shapes and zod is not in the locked runtime dependency set (BD7); hand-rolled validators keep every validation shape in the one schema home (guard-rail)
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.780Z — chose **BD13: MCP server launches via `harness mcp [--root <dir>]` on the existing commander CLI; SPEC §CLI extended one line; .mcp.json snippet {mcpServers:{harness:{command:"harness",args:["mcp"]}}} emitted by src/mcp/register.ts, wired into `harness init` in Phase 4** over a separate bin entry (harness-mcp); improvising an undocumented surface without extending SPEC because one bin keeps the install/registration surface minimal; SPEC did not list the command, so a one-line SPEC extension beats improvisation
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.841Z — chose **BD14: Projections seam built in Phase 2 — regenerateProjections(initiativeDir, state) renders minimal v0 plan.md + decisions.md via template functions; every tool append calls it; generated files carry a "do not hand-edit" header; Phase 3 extends the templates in place** over deferring projections entirely to Phase 3; building full templates in Phase 2 because deferral would violate the SPEC tool contract (every tool regenerates projections) and Phase 2 acceptance; full templates now would be Phase 3 scope creep
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.902Z — chose **BD15: Session semantics — the stdio server process holds ONE in-memory active session: start_session mints a ulid, appends session_started, sets it active; subsequent appends use the active id/tool with actor "agent"; end_session's session_id arg wins over the active session and the slot clears after the append** over a multi-session registry because one agent process per stdio server — YAGNI
- 2026-07-07T10:53:44.964Z — chose **BD16: Initiative resolution — explicit `initiative` arg wins; else current git branch → slug via .harness/bindings.json, branch read by parsing .git/HEAD (following worktree-style gitdir files); detached HEAD/no repo/no binding → typed unknown_initiative error; resolved slugs must exist under .harness/initiatives/ (typos must not create logs); repo root = server cwd, `harness mcp --root` overrides** over spawning `git rev-parse` because a subprocess per call, needs git on PATH, and HEAD parsing is sufficient for v1
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.025Z — chose **BD17: Typed tool errors — failures return isError:true with content[0].text = JSON {code, message, errors?}; code union lives in ONE place (@harness/schema/tool-inputs): invalid_input | unknown_initiative | unknown_tool | unknown_event | io_error; validation failures carry field-level errors; no event is appended on failure** over MCP protocol errors (McpError) because agents see tool-result text, not protocol faults — in-band typed errors are actionable and parseable
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.090Z — chose **BD18: Tool arg → payload mapping at the tool boundary — update_task {task_id, status, note?} → task_status_changed {id, status, note?}; update_plan {plan} validated by the existing PlanStructure validator via validatePayload; log_decision → decision_logged; add_note → note_added** over changing either frozen contract (SPEC tool signature says task_id, Phase 1 payload schema says id) because mapping at the boundary keeps both contracts frozen; reusing validatePayload means tool input and event payload cannot drift
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.153Z — chose **BD19: Total-jurisdiction protocol block (user field finding, Jul 4) — SPEC §CLI init contract expanded: the installed block MUST state (a) all work state lives in harness records, never tool memory or scratch files; (b) work matching no existing initiative requires `harness new` first; (c) bindings resolve which record a session serves. Affects tasks 4.1 and 5.1. The same hand-edit reverted SPEC §Repo layout/§Event types to pre-monorepo text and dropped the `harness mcp` line — restored** over per-file jurisdiction only because dogfooding showed a second initiative's state leaking into Claude Code native memory + a scratch dir — per-file jurisdiction is not enough
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.214Z — chose **BD20: Session identity correlation through the log, no side channel — the hook appends session_started with envelope.session = Claude's session_id; harness_start_session ADOPTS the newest open session for the initiative (no duplicate append), minting a fresh ulid only when none is open; the Stop shim verifies write-back by folding the log for Claude's session_id (adoption heuristic later REMOVED by BD43)** over a side-channel correlation file (second source of truth beside the log); passing session ids through env vars because the log is the one source of truth; env vars are not available to the MCP server process
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.278Z — chose **BD21: New event type session_closed {reason} — the mechanical close from the SessionEnd hook: fold sets session.ended only (never summary/next_action, never overrides an earlier end), warns + skips unregistered sessions (no stubs); SPEC §Event types updated; SPEC §State sessions gained model?/next_action?** over reusing session_ended with a placeholder summary because a fabricated summary would clobber the fold-derived current.next_action and defeat the Stop shim's write-back check
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.339Z — chose **BD22: Best-effort hooks — every `harness event` handler exits 0 silently on any failure; only Stop deliberately exits 2, and only for a registered-but-unwritten session (stop_hook_active → 0 loop guard; unregistered session_id → 0 so foreign repos are never blocked; write-back check = fold-derived session.summary present); SessionStart re-fires skip the duplicate append but still print the status block; handlers are pure functions returning {exitCode, stdout, stderr}** over erroring loudly because a memory layer that can break sessions in unbound repos is worse than no memory layer; thin commander wiring keeps tests free of process.exit
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.399Z — chose **BD23: PostToolUse mapping — Edit|MultiEdit → file_touched {path, op:"edit"}, Write → op:"write", Bash → command_run {cmd}; envelope source "hook", actor "agent", session = stdin session_id (fallback "cli"); unknown tool_name or missing file_path/command → exit 0, append nothing** over recording tool_response; an op per hook tool name because tool_response is verbose with no state value; the record cares about mutation kind (edit|write), not which editor variant did it
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.463Z — chose **BD24: Status projection ≤10k strategy — fixed per-section char budgets (goal 600, next_action/blocked 500, session summary 1200, 5 decisions × 280, phase list ≤12 lines; worst case ~6.5k) plus a final enforceStatusLimit slice to 10,000 with a truncation notice; the status block is stdout-only (no status.md file); sessions/<id>.md regenerated for every known session on every append with filenames sanitized ([^A-Za-z0-9._-] → _)** over proportional (single-pool) budgeting because fixed per-section caps are easier to reason about for worst cases; session ids come from outside, so hostile ids must stay inside sessions/
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.525Z — chose **BD25: `harness init` mechanics — shim sources ship INSIDE the bundled CLI (esbuild sh-as-text loader; vitest mirrors it); byte-level idempotency via write-if-changed; ownership rules: shims harness-owned (kept current, 0755), repo.md hand-written (create-only), CLAUDE.md protocol block installs once between <!-- harness:protocol --> markers and is never touched again (hand edits inside survive); settings.json/.mcp.json merged with existing entries respected; unparseable user JSON aborts init exit 1** over reading shims from the package dir; JSON-format-preserving edits because package-dir reads break packaging (only dist/ ships); idempotency only needs OUR formatting to be stable; merging must never risk a clobber
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.587Z — chose **BD26: `harness new`/`switch` semantics — CLI-created events carry actor "human", source "cli", session "cli"; new resolves the branch BEFORE creating anything so a bind failure leaves the repo untouched; --no-bind is the detached-HEAD/no-repo escape hatch; omitted --goal → non-empty placeholder; bindings.json writes are read-modify-write merges; switch refuses unknown slugs (typos must not create logs — BD16 extended to the CLI)** over binding "worktree name" instead of branch because SPEC §Record layout keys bindings by branch-or-worktree and currentBranch() already resolves worktree HEADs to their branch
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.647Z — chose **BD27: `harness status` render lives in src/projections/templates/status.ts as renderFullStatus beside the capped renderStatus — same fold, uncapped, per-task tree ([x]/[~]/[!]/[ ]); fold warnings → stderr with exit 0; resolution failures exit 1 with the BD16 typed message + usage hint** over reusing the capped renderStatus for the CLI; putting the render in src/cli/ because the 10k cap is a SessionStart context budget (BD3), not a terminal constraint — caps would silently hide tasks; templates are the only template home (guard-rail); a corrupt line must not hide status
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.705Z — chose **BD28: export/import both take an optional [slug] positional resolving like status (explicit wins, else branch binding, BD16); SPEC §CLI extended to match; import summary is one parseable JSON line {"appended":N,"skipped":M}; stream warnings → stderr; projections regenerate only when appended > 0; handlers take the already-read stream** over an export command that can only serve the bound initiative because the BD19 field finding showed moving a second initiative's log is a real workflow; an idempotent re-import rewrites nothing; commander owns file/stdin IO so tests stay process-free
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.768Z — chose **BD29: `harness serve` — node:http only, bound 127.0.0.1 ONLY, three routes (/state, /state/<slug>, /events SSE; non-GET 405, else 404, slugs validated [a-z0-9-]+); chokidar watches events.jsonl by basename, re-folds just the changed initiative, pushes state (measured 51ms vs ≤500ms contract); startServer factory awaits watcher-ready, close() releases everything; 15s heartbeat; a fold error degrades to empty state for that slug, not a 500; renderFullStatus gained a "Files touched" section during Phase 4 acceptance** over express (dependency law); any static/UI route (scope law); debouncing appends because the localhost-JSON-only law and locked dependency set forbid them; the latency budget is generous and consumers coalesce; an endpoint that dies on one torn log is useless
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.828Z — chose **BD30: `harness event append` — the convention-dialect surface: --type/--payload required, --session/--source/--actor flags, optional [slug] positional (BD16); NOT best-effort (BD22 exemption) — any failure exits 1 with BD17 typed-error JSON on stderr and appends NOTHING; success prints {ok, event_id}; all writes route through appendAndProject (validate → append → regenerate); a dialect session_ended satisfies the Stop hook identically to the MCP path** over a top-level `harness append`; stdin payloads because an explicit caller deserves real errors; BD4 fixed the name and grouping under `event` keeps the machine surface in one place; one-line flag invocations are what convention-following agents compose reliably
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.890Z — chose **BD31: AGENTS.md protocol block — init installs AGENTS_PROTOCOL_BLOCK with the SAME marker discipline as CLAUDE.md (same markers, create-if-missing, append-if-unmarked, never touched once markers exist); carries the three BD19 clauses but a CLI-only loop (orient via `harness status`, write via `harness event append`, write-back MANDATORY) plus prohibitions: never hand-edit projections, never edit events.jsonl, corrections are new correction events; SPEC §CLI init line extended** over reusing PROTOCOL_BLOCK verbatim; different marker names per file because AGENTS.md readers (OpenCode, Codex, plain shells) cannot be assumed to have MCP or a blocking Stop hook — the MANDATORY clause is the compensating control; the MCP loop names harness_* tools an MCP-less tool cannot call; one discipline keeps hand-edits surviving in both
- 2026-07-07T10:53:45.952Z — chose **BD32: OpenCode adapter = docs + simulated checklist, no plugin code — docs/opencode-adapter.md maps the hook surface to OpenCode plugin hooks (tool.execute.after ≈ PostToolUse via shell-out) and states the gap explicitly: OpenCode has NO exit-2-blocking Stop equivalent, so write-back rests on the AGENTS.md MANDATORY clause; a future plugin is specified as thin shell-outs only (BD4); the §3 12-step manual checklist is simulated 1:1 against the BUILT CLI in acceptance.phase5.test.ts; 5.2's checkbox stays open until the manual run happens** over building the plugin now; checking the box on the simulation alone because OpenCode's plugin API is outside the locked dependency window and the convention dialect is the contracted v1 fallback (SPEC §Acceptance Phase 5); the simulation proves checklist ACCURACY, not agent compliance — the honest record beats the checkmark
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.013Z — chose **BD33: serve also pushes on addDir of a new initiative directory, with a bounded wait (20×50ms) for its events.jsonl before folding (then pushes whatever exists); the SPEC 500ms SLA still asserted for appends to existing logs; the new-dir test asserts the push happens, not its speed** over usePolling; widening test timeouts alone because a dir created and populated in one burst can lose the file add event to a chokidar/fsevents race (reproduced ~1-in-5 flake); polling is constant cost for a first-push-only race; the miss was real product behavior, not test tightness
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.075Z — chose **BD34: Protocol CLAUDE.md moved docs/ → repo root (ceremony prep, Jul 7); internal references path-fixed; SPEC §Repo layout updated** over a thin root pointer file to docs/CLAUDE.md because Claude Code auto-loads only a root CLAUDE.md and the 5.3 cold resume "from the record alone" needs that routing; two protocol files drift — one file, one home
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.138Z — chose **BD35: Root AGENTS.md added as a THIN ROUTER (Jul 7, after the Codex cold start failed) pointing at docs/harness.md + docs/SPEC.md and deferring all rules to CLAUDE.md (single source); field note: a session must LAUNCH inside the repo — no root file can route a blank workspace** over a full protocol copy (one appeared untracked and was reshaped) because Codex/OpenCode-family tools auto-read AGENTS.md, not CLAUDE.md — BD34 fixed routing for Claude Code only; duplicated rules drift (the exact reason BD34 rejected pointer+copy pairs); both cold-resume dry runs proved agents follow multi-hop routing reliably
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.198Z — chose **BD36: serve gained a bounded reconciliation safety net (Jul 7 Codex resume) — while SSE clients are connected, the server scans initiative log size/mtime every 250ms and broadcasts any changed log the watcher missed; the watcher remains the fast path; versions seeded after watcher-ready; no scanning without clients; JSON-only localhost scope and the SPEC 500ms existing-append SLA preserved** over unbounded polling; static/UI work; treating the failure as test-only because even the BD33 addDir fallback can miss a create+populate burst on fsevents (full `npm test` reproduced it 234/235); the failure reproduced the exact product path the test protects
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.258Z — chose **BD37: Phase 6 (hardening + distribution readiness) added post-plan on user direction ("continue the development", Jul 7) — README, installable tarball, atomic projection writes, version single-sourcing, repo.md surfacing; scope law intact (no UI/sync/team/telemetry)** over starting post-v1 roadmap items (ui, sync) because the contents are the gap between "SPEC-complete" and "shippable"; ui/sync would be a guard-rail violation inside the window — those need the user to reopen scope explicitly
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.320Z — chose **BD38: Projection writes are atomic — every generated file is written to a dot-prefixed `.<target>.<pid>.<random>.tmp` in the SAME directory then renameSync over the target (atomic on POSIX same-fs); write failures remove the temp; a unit test asserts no *.tmp lingers; temp names never collide and serve's basename-filtered watcher never fires on them; generator keeps plain-overwrite semantics (init idempotency never depended on it)** over write-if-changed in the generator; fsync ceremony because serve's /state fold, a SessionStart fold, or a human tailing plan.md must never observe a half-written projection; write-if-changed is a read per file per append to optimize nothing measured; events.jsonl is truth — projections are regenerable
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.384Z — chose **BD39: CLI version single-sourced — src/cli/index.ts imports `version` from ../../package.json (esbuild inlines the named JSON import) and passes it to program.version(); the hardcoded "0.1.0" literal is gone; asserted in the built-CLI smoke: `--version` output equals package.json's field at test time, so a bump can never drift** over reading package.json at runtime relative to the bin because only dist/ ships inside a tarball whose package.json location differs between npm layouts — inlining at build time has no runtime path to get wrong
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.444Z — chose **BD40: Repo memory in the SessionStart context — .harness/repo.md surfaces as a "Repo memory (.harness/repo.md)" section after goal/current, before the phase tree, with its OWN 1,500-char budget and a line-structure-preserving clip (clipBlock) + truncation pointer; missing/unreadable/empty/byte-identical-init-stub → section omitted; IO + stub check in the handler, budget/placement/rendering in templates/status.ts; the global ≤10k guard re-proven with a ~50k repo.md; SPEC §Hooks SessionStart line extended** over folding repo.md into the goal budget; rendering it in `harness status` too because repo-scoped memory must not compete with initiative state; the uncapped CLI print is initiative-scoped — repo.md is a context-injection concern (revisit on demand)
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.504Z — chose **BD41: Engine manifest reclassified for bundled-bin distribution — ALL packages/engine dependencies (incl. private @harness/schema, which made the old tarball uninstallable) moved to devDependencies because dist/cli.js is fully esbuild-bundled and consumers need ZERO runtime deps; "prepack": "node build.mjs"; the engine STAYS "private": true until the user green-lights publishing (npm pack works with private); guarded by test/packaging.test.ts: npm pack → global install into temp prefix → --version answers, zero deps installed, init → new → status drive in a fixture (~1s warm)** over publishing @harness/schema to make the old manifest installable; removing "private" now because the BD7 dependency LAW is about the USED set, which is byte-identical (still imported, still bundled); publishing an internal package nobody imports at runtime is pointless; publish is a user decision, not a build gate
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.565Z — chose **BD42: README.md at repo root — terse/factual npm front door in the docs' register (no marketing voice): what harness is, honest pre-publish install, quickstart, the three integration surfaces, the SPEC §Record layout diagram verbatim, a CLI table, doc links; the tarball README is ONE source at root — engine "prepack" copies it in, "postpack" deletes it, .gitignore excludes it so it can never drift; packaging E2E asserts the installed package carries it and the engine dir is clean after pack** over a hand-maintained packages/engine/README.md; symlinking because two front doors drift (the BD34/BD35 no-pointer+copy rule, kept by generating the copy at pack time only); npm pack follows symlinks inconsistently across platforms
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.627Z — chose **BD43: Adopt-by-id sessions — harness_start_session gained an OPTIONAL session_id arg and the BD20 newest-open adoption heuristic is REMOVED ENTIRELY; session_id naming an OPEN session → adopt exactly it; an ENDED session → typed invalid_input with zero appends; UNKNOWN id → session_started appended with that id (registers it); omitted → fresh ulid, NEVER adopts; the SessionStart context now opens with "Session: <id> — pass this as session_id" (id clipped to 120 chars; ≤10k cap untouched); init's CLAUDE.md protocol START step updated; SPEC §MCP tools + §Hooks updated; all newest-open tests converted + new branch coverage** over keeping newest-open as a fallback when session_id is omitted because the heuristic cross-adopted: a second agent's start_session silently took over the first agent's open session, so parallel sessions corrupted each other's attribution and Stop gates — the fallback IS the bug; an omitting agent gets its own identity and unwritten hook sessions surface via the 7.2 derived resume
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.688Z — chose **BD44: Derived resume fallback — fold aggregates per-session activity from mechanical events by envelope.session: activity? = {files[] deduped first-touch order, commands count, task_changes[] "<id> → <status>"} capped at 20 + "+N more" sentinel; attached ONLY when ≥1 such event, ONLY to registered sessions, NEVER session "cli"; corrections void activity; closed_reason recorded when session_closed set ended; renderStatus renders a derived resume block for the newest summary-less session with activity unless a written-back session is newer; sessions/<id>.md gained an Activity section; describeActivity in templates/shared.ts; state shape in engine fold.ts (not payload schema); SPEC §State updated** over aggregating into unregistered stub sessions; surfacing the derived line in renderFullStatus/`harness status` because a resume point for a session nobody registered is noise (same rule as BD21); the design scoped surfaces to the status context block + sessions/<id>.md — the CLI print already lists files_touched (revisit on demand)
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.748Z — chose **BD45: `harness uninit [--purge]` — surgical inverse of init: unlink exactly the four shims (dirs removed only when THIS run emptied them); settings.json pruned by shim-path substring across ALL event keys (wrapped/moved commands still match), with emptied groups → arrays → hooks key pruned in order; .mcp.json loses mcpServers.harness; CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md lose the marker block + exactly one blank-line seam (missing end marker → warn, file untouched); unparseable JSON aborts exit 1; .harness/ KEPT by default ("use --purge to delete"), --purge rm -rfs it naming `harness export` as backup; managed-file deletion ONLY under --purge and only when THIS run emptied the file (resolves the byte-clean round-trip conflict, smallest deviation)** over an install manifest tracking init provenance; preserving arbitrary user JSON formatting; keeping emptied managed files even under --purge because a manifest is a second source of truth about what is installed; init already normalizes merged files to stable JSON so stable-formatted files round-trip byte-exact; "never delete managed files" cannot coexist with the contracted byte-clean fresh-repo init → uninit --purge round-trip
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.808Z — chose **BD46: `harness adopt <legacy-file> [slug] [--mark]` — the CLI mechanizes environment checks + marking and prints THE MIGRATION BRIEF; it never parses the legacy markdown; validation order with BD17 typed errors (file exists, .harness/ exists naming `harness init`, initiative resolves with a "create it with `harness new`" hint); the brief is self-contained and verbatim-executable: five dialect templates sharing ONE `migration-<ulid>` session id, a Step 0 session_started {"tool":"migration"} registration (added during 8.3 — without it the session_ended folds into a stub session and every later status warns), all --actor human (transcription is human-authored truth, BD26 precedent); then the repo-knowledge move into .harness/repo.md, the protocol-retirement checklist, and the verification line; --mark prepends an idempotent superseded banner between <!-- harness:superseded --> markers; paths render repo-relative; SPEC §CLI gained the adopt line** over freeform markdown parsing; appending migration events directly from the CLI; requiring --mark before the replay because parsing is fragile against hand-maintained prose — an agent executes the brief and transcribes, the CLI carries the protocol (BD4 philosophy); the CLI cannot transcribe what it refuses to parse; marking belongs AFTER status reproduces the legacy state
- 2026-07-07T10:53:46.869Z — chose **BD47: Self-host the harness repo on its own record — `harness init` + `harness new harness-build` in this repo, replay docs/harness.md (plan, BD1–BD46, repo knowledge) into the event record via the `harness adopt` migration brief, and retire the dual prose protocol from CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md; docs/harness.md becomes an archived pre-migration artifact stamped with the superseded banner (ceremony artifacts — session log, arm-C resume, field findings — remain readable there)** over continuing the prose protocol in docs/harness.md alongside the shipped engine because the engine now exists and `harness adopt` was built for exactly this migration; the repo should be a living demo of its own product
